column1: sriracha — general alternative with no constraints
column6: {"note":"These alternatives help replace sriracha’s spicy, garlicky, tangy heat with options commonly found in North American stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Huy Fong Chili Garlic Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A chili-garlic hot sauce with a similar peppery, garlicky kick and medium heat, though it is chunkier than classic sriracha.","where":"Asian foods aisle or hot sauce aisle"},{"name_en":"Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"Another authentic sriracha-style sauce with the same sweet-heat profile and pourable texture.","where":"Asian foods aisle"},{"name_en":"Tabasco Sriracha Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A familiar sriracha-style condiment with a slightly sharper vinegar-forward heat but the same general use case.","where":"Hot sauce aisle"},{"name_en":"Flying Goose Sriracha Hot Chili Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A classic Thai-style sriracha with garlic, sweetness, and heat that closely matches the original flavor profile.","where":"Asian foods aisle"}]}

column1: butter + lactose intolerant — all alts must be dairy-free
column6: {"note":"These alternatives avoid dairy lactose while covering common butter uses in North American stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Olive oil spread","match":"Similar","desc":"A dairy-free spread made from plant oils that can be used on toast and for many cooking tasks.","where":"Refrigerated dairy-free spread section"},{"name_en":"Vegan buttery stick","match":"Similar","desc":"A plant-based butter alternative designed for baking, sautéing, and spreading with a butter-like texture.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based butter section"},{"name_en":"Coconut oil spread","match":"Similar","desc":"A dairy-free spread with a mild buttery profile that works well for cooking and baking.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based spread section"},{"name_en":"Ghee-free olive oil cooking spread","match":"Similar","desc":"A dairy-free cooking spread made from vegetable oils that replaces butter in sautéing and pan use.","where":"Refrigerated margarine/spreads section"}]}
column22: Evaluating against the rubric criteria: 1) All alternatives dairy-free/lactose-free: Earth Balance (vegan, dairy-free ✓), Miyoko's (vegan, dairy-free ✓), Coconut Oil (dairy-free ✓), Ghee - this is problematic. Ghee is derived from dairy butter. While it's virtually lactose-free, it's not fully dairy-free and some lactose-intolerant individuals may still react. However, ghee is widely accepted as safe for lactose intolerant people since the milk solids (containing lactose) are removed. This is borderline but generally acceptable. 2) No regular margarine suggested without checking dairy content - correct, no margarine is suggested. 3) Match levels honesty: The rubric states plant-based butters should be 'Similar' not 'Very close' for baking. Both Earth Balance and Miyoko's are listed as 'Very close' which the rubric explicitly says is not honest for baking purposes. Plant-based butters behave differently in baking due to different fat content and water content. 4) The note acknowledges the lactose constraint - yes, it mentions lactose and digestive issues for lactose intolerant individuals. The main issue is the match levels for plant-based butters being rated 'Very close' when the rubric explicitly states they should be 'Similar' for baking. This is a clear violation of the rubric's requirement for honest match levels.

column1: rice vinegar — general substitution for acidic condiment
column6: {"note":"These alternatives help replace rice vinegar’s mild acidity and subtle sweetness in North American grocery stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Seasoned rice vinegar","match":"Very close","desc":"Rice vinegar with added sugar and salt, so it tastes very close to regular rice vinegar but is a bit less neutral.","where":"Vinegar aisle / Asian foods aisle"},{"name_en":"White wine vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"A mild, light vinegar that can stand in for rice vinegar in dressings, marinades, and quick pickles.","where":"Vinegar aisle"},{"name_en":"Champagne vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Delicate and less sharp than many other vinegars, making it one of the closest Western-style substitutes.","where":"Vinegar aisle / gourmet condiments"},{"name_en":"Apple cider vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Slightly fruitier and stronger than rice vinegar, but still works well when you want gentle acidity.","where":"Vinegar aisle"}]}

column1: chicken breast + vegetarian — all alts must be meat-free
column6: {"note":"Vegetarian alternatives to chicken breast should be plant-based proteins that can replace it in meals while staying fully compliant.","alts":[{"name_en":"Extra-firm tofu","match":"Different but works","desc":"A versatile high-protein soy option that can be sliced, cubed, or grilled in place of chicken breast.","where":"Refrigerated tofu section, near produce or plant-based proteins"},{"name_en":"Tempeh","match":"Different but works","desc":"A firm fermented soy cake with a hearty texture that works well in stir-fries, sandwiches, and grilled dishes.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based proteins section"},{"name_en":"Seitan","match":"Different but works","desc":"A wheat-gluten protein with a chewy, meat-like texture that is especially good for sautéing, grilling, or slicing.","where":"Refrigerated vegan meat alternatives or freezer section"},{"name_en":"Plant-based chicken strips","match":"Different but works","desc":"A ready-to-cook meat alternative designed to mimic chicken in salads, wraps, and skillet meals.","where":"Frozen plant-based meat section"}]}

column1: fish sauce + Korean taste — cuisine-appropriate alternatives
column6: {"note":"These alternatives help recreate the salty, savory depth of fish sauce in Korean-style cooking using products commonly found in North American grocery stores.","alts":[{"name_en":"Aekjeot (Korean fish sauce)","match":"Very close","desc":"This is the most authentic Korean substitute because it is the same category of fermented fish sauce, often made from anchovy or sand lance.","where":"Asian foods aisle, Korean section"},{"name_en":"Red Boat Fish Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A high-quality traditional fish sauce with a clean, umami-forward flavor that works well in Korean marinades, soups, and dipping sauces.","where":"Asian foods aisle or international condiments"},{"name_en":"Squid Brand Fish Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A widely available Southeast Asian fish sauce that delivers the same salty, fermented umami profile used in many savory dishes.","where":"Asian foods aisle or international condiments"},{"name_en":"Golden Mountain Seasoning Sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"This Thai-style seasoning sauce is less fishy but adds salty umami depth that can stand in for some of fish sauce’s role in cooking.","where":"Asian foods aisle or sauces/condiments"}]}
